methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $43M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy

data.narrative:
Renaissance Fusion is a Series A-stage nuclear energy company that has raised $43M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 peers across nuclear energy: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow comparables. Within the same stage and valuation tier, only one same-stage peer and one adjacent-stage peer were available in the nuclear energy sector—below the three-datapoint threshold required for reliable cross-sectional analysis. A stage-progression method was therefore applied, using the $43M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage step-ups, yielding $86M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $73M to $99M, with a median of $86M. This range reflects limited triangulation to a single method and carries material uncertainty around appropriate stage-based step-up multiples in the early-stage nuclear sector. The scarcity of same-stage nuclear comparables introduces model risk; future valuation refinement would benefit from public market data on peer multiples (revenue, capacity, or capex intensity) once Renaissance Fusion reaches measurable operational scale.
